title: 10 best herbal teas for palpitation
intro_desc: Herbal teas for palpitation are caffeine-free, plant-based beverages that can help manage symptoms of irregular heartbeat or palpitation. These teas are often made from flowers, leaves, seeds, or roots of various plants, and they can have a calming effect on the body. Some examples of herbal teas that are beneficial for palpitation include chamomile, lavender, passionflower, and valerian root. These teas work by promoting relaxation, reducing anxiety, and decreasing heart rate, which can all contribute to a decrease in palpitations. By incorporating these herbal teas into their daily routine, individuals can experience a significant improvement in their quality of life, as they can help reduce anxiety and promote a sense of calm and well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Common hawthorn teas helps with palpitation because it contains bioactive compounds that have a positive impact on the heart. Hawthorn's flavonoids, oligomeric proanthocyanidins, and anthrocyanosides help to dilate blood vessels, improve blood flow, and lower blood pressure, thereby reducing cardiac workload and alleviating palpitations. Additionally, hawthorn's anti-inflammatory properties may also contribute to its ability to calm the heart and reduce symptoms of palpitation.
remedy_properties: Cardiotonic activity: Hawthorn tea has been shown to have a positive effect on heart function, which can help alleviate palpitations by improving blood flow and reducing strain on the heart.

Antioxidant activity: Hawthorn's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system, which can contribute to palpitation symptoms such as irregular heartbeat or arrhythmia.
Vasodilatory effect: Hawthorn tea has been found to dilate blood vessels, which can help reduce blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of palpitations such as rapid or pounding heartbeats.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of hawthorn are rich in flavonoids and anthocyanins, which help to strengthen the heart and alleviate palpitations by promoting blood vessel relaxation.

Bark: Hawthorn bark contains a significant amount of quercetin, a potent antioxidant that helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system, relieving palpitation symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried leaves and flowers of crataegus monogyna.
Clean and chop the harvested plant material into smaller pieces.
Combine the chopped herb with boiling water in a teapot.
Strain the tea mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Serve the prepared hawthorn tea hot or allow it to cool for later consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Midland hawthorn teas helps with palpitation because its unique blend of bioflavonoids, flavonoids, and anthocyanins work synergistically to relax and calm the heart. The tea's flavonoid content helps to dilate blood vessels, reducing blood pressure and alleviating stress on the cardiovascular system. Additionally, the tea's antioxidant properties help to protect the heart from damage caused by free radicals, promoting a sense of well-being and reducing palpitation symptoms.
remedy_properties: Oleuropein: A polyphenol that helps to dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing the workload on the heart, which can help alleviate palpitations.

Flavonoids: Compounds found in hawthorn berries that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system, contributing to palpitation relief.
Quercetin: A flavonoid that has a direct impact on heart rhythm, helping to stabilize heartbeat and alleviate irregularities, including palpitations.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Midland Hawthorn plant contain flavonoids and terpenoids that help to calm the heart and reduce palpitations by promoting relaxation and reducing stress.
Bark: The bark of the Midland Hawthorn plant is used to prepare a tea that helps to soothe the nerves, calm the heart, and alleviate palpitation symptoms caused by anxiety or nervousness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried midland hawthorn leaves flowers and berries in the early morning or late evening.
Rinse 10-15 grams of the harvested plant material with clean water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine the rinsed plant material with boiling water in a large pot for 10-15 minutes to create a strong infusion.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a heat-resistant pitcher or teapot, discarding solids.
Allow the tea to steep at room temperature for an additional 30-60 minutes before serving hot or cold with sugar and honey optional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop teas helps with palpitations because it contains a unique combination of herbs that work together to calm the nervous system. The Passiflora incarnata, also known as passionflower, is a natural sedative that has been used for centuries to treat anxiety and insomnia. When combined with other herbal ingredients, maypop tea can help slow down an overactive heart rate and promote relaxation, reducing palpitation symptoms and promoting overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Cardiotonic and vasodilatory properties: Maypop tea's cardiotonic effects help to regulate heart rhythm and improve blood flow, reducing palpitation symptoms by promoting a healthy heartbeat.

Antispasmodic properties: Maypop's antispasmodic compounds relax the smooth muscle tissue in the heart, which can help to alleviate palpitations caused by spasms or irregular contractions of the heart muscle.

Anxiolytic and stress-reducing effects: Maypop tea contains flavonoids that have been shown to have anxiolytic properties, helping to reduce stress and anxiety levels, which are common triggers for palpitations.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in cardiac glycosides, which help regulate heart rate and alleviate palpitations.
Bark: Containing flavonoids and tannins, it has a calming effect on the cardiovascular system, reducing palpitation symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh maypop flowers and leaves from a trusted source or store-dried herbs.
Measure one teaspoon of dried maypop flowers and leaves for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Combine the measured maypop herb with boiling water in a large glass teapot or infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes, depending on desired tea strength.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth and serve immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm teas helps with palpitation because it contains rosmarinic acid, a compound that has a calming effect on the nervous system. This acidity helps to reduce stress and anxiety levels, which are often triggers for palpitations. Additionally, lemon balm's sedative properties can help to slow down an irregular heartbeat and promote a sense of relaxation, making it easier for the heart to return to a normal rhythm.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Lemon balm tea contains high levels of antioxidants, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, thereby alleviating palpitation symptoms such as rapid heartbeat and chest tightness.

Cardiotonic compounds: Lemon balm tea is rich in cardiotonic compounds like rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, which have been shown to improve cardiovascular function by reducing blood pressure, heart rate, and cardiac contractility, thus helping to regulate palpitations.

Anxiolytic and sedative properties: Lemon balm tea has a calming effect on the nervous system due to its anxiolytic and sedative compounds like citral and geraniol, which can help to reduce anxiety and stress-related palpitation symptoms by promoting relaxation and reducing sympathetic nervous activity.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm are often used in herbal teas for palpitation because they contain rosmarinic acid, which helps to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety.
Flowers: Lemon balm flowers are added to teas for palpitation due to their soothing effects on the heart and blood vessels, helping to regulate irregular heartbeat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried melissa officinalis leaves and flowers of good quality and potency.
Measure one teaspoon of dried lemon balm leaves per eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for five to seven minutes in a ceramic or glass teapot to release essential oils.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth to remove solids completely.
Enjoy the soothing and calming herbal lemon balm tea immediately, served hot or iced as desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian teas helps with palpitation because it has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help to calm an overactive heart. The calming properties of valerian tea work by interacting with GABA receptors in the brain, reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, which can alleviate symptoms of palpitation such as rapid heartbeat and irregular rhythms. Additionally, valerian tea's relaxing effects can also help to reduce stress and promote overall well-being, further contributing to its benefits for those experiencing palpitation.
remedy_properties: Sedative properties: Valerian tea helps to calm the nervous system, reducing feelings of anxiety and stress that can contribute to palpitations.
Cardiotonic properties: Valerian tea has been shown to improve blood flow and reduce blood pressure, which can help regulate heart rhythm and alleviate palpitations.
Antioxidant properties: Valerian tea contains antioxidants that help protect the heart from oxidative damage and inflammation, which can contribute to palpitations and other cardiovascular issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots have a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps to alleviate palpitations and promote relaxation.
Rhyzomes: Valerian rhyzomes contain valepotriates, compounds that help to reduce anxiety and stress, which can contribute to palpitations.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried valeriana officinalis roots or rhizomes for a total of one ounce.
Combine the valerian root with one quart of boiling water in a large teapot.
Steep the mixture for ten to fifteen minutes to allow the flavors and active compounds to meld.
Strain the tea into a separate container using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Serve the tea hot, allowing it to cool slightly before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap teas helps with palpitation because it possesses a unique blend of flavonoids, saponins, and essential oils that work together to calm the nervous system. The tea's active compounds, including baicalin and scutellarein, have been shown to reduce anxiety and stress levels by interacting with neurotransmitters like GABA and serotonin, leading to a sense of relaxation and reduced heart palpitation symptoms. This soothing effect helps regulate heart rhythm, promoting a more tranquil and stable heartbeat.
remedy_properties: Baicalin: Baicalin, a flavonoid glycoside, helps to reduce palpitations by exhibiting anti-anxiety and sedative effects, which can alleviate stress-related heart conditions.

Scutellarein: Scutellarein, another flavonoid compound, has been shown to decrease the frequency of atrial fibrillation (a type of irregular heartbeat) in animal studies, indicating its potential for reducing palpitations.

Volatile oils: The volatile oils present in skullcap tea, such as borneol and camphor, have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to regulate heart rate and rhythm, thereby alleviating palpitations.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the skullcap plant contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds responsible for its sedative and anti-anxiety properties, making them effective in calming palpitations.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of skullcap have been traditionally used to prepare medicinal teas that help regulate heart rate and blood pressure, alleviating symptoms of palpitation.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried scutellaria lateriflora flowers for every eight ounces of boiling water needed.
Steep the dried flowers in boiling water for ten to fifteen minutes to allow for proper infusion and extraction.
Strain the tea into a cup using a piece of cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer to remove any loose herb particles.
Discard the solids and add honey or other sweetener to taste if desired to enhance flavor and palatability.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving hot or chilling it in the refrigerator for later consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Littleleaf linden teas helps with palpitation because it contains flavonoids, which have a calming effect on the heart and blood vessels. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers of palpitations. Additionally, littleleaf linden tea's ability to lower blood pressure and improve circulation can further alleviate symptoms of palpitations. Overall, its soothing effects can provide relief from fluttering or racing hearts and promote a sense of calm and well-being.
remedy_properties: Calming and Sedative Properties: Littleleaf Linden tea has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps to alleviate palpitations by reducing stress and anxiety.

Antioxidant Properties: The antioxidants present in Littleleaf Linden tea help to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to palpitation-related conditions such as hypertension and tachycardia.

Vasodilatory Properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids in Littleleaf Linden tea have been found to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, which can help to reduce palpitations by decreasing blood pressure and heart rate.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and saponins, which help to calm the heart and reduce palpitations.
Bark: Containing phenolic compounds, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of palpitation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of tilia cordata from a pesticide-free garden or store-bought dried leaves in airtight containers.
Rinse freshly harvested leaves with clean water and pat dry with paper towels to remove excess moisture.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried leaves or 2 teaspoons of fresh leaves with 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl, discarding solids and retaining liquid extract.
Allow the tea to cool and steep in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours or overnight before serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Large-leaved lime teas helps with palpitation because its unique blend of herbs, rich in calcium, magnesium, and potassium, works to calm and regulate the heart's rhythm. The calming effects of these minerals help to ease tension in the cardiovascular system, reducing the frequency and severity of palpitations. Additionally, the tea's soothing properties can help to promote relaxation and reduce stress levels, which are common triggers for palpitations. By drinking herbal large-leaved lime teas regularly, individuals may experience a significant reduction in palpitation episodes and improved overall heart health.
remedy_properties: Cardiac glycosides: These compounds help to regulate heartbeat and reduce palpitations by slowing down the heart rate and increasing its efficiency, allowing the heart to pump more effectively.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in large-leaved lime tea, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system, which can contribute to palpitations.
Catechins: Catechins, a type of polyphenol found in large-leaved lime tea, have been shown to have anti-arrhythmic effects, helping to regulate abnormal heart rhythms that can cause palpitations.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots are effective in reducing palpitation symptoms by calming the nervous system.
Bark: Containing anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, bark helps to soothe cardiac arrhythmias and alleviate palpitation discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh tilia platyphyllos leaves from your garden or local market in late spring or early summer for best flavor and aroma.
Rinse the collected leaves with clean water to remove any dirt or debris then pat them dry with a paper towel.
Combine 20-30 grams of dried tilia platyphyllos leaves with 1 liter of boiling water in a large ceramic teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes depending on your desired strength and flavor profile allowing the herbs to infuse fully.
Strain the tea into individual cups through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids then serve hot with honey if desired.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American basswood teas helps with palpitation because it contains compounds that have a calming effect on the nervous system, soothing irregular heartbeats and reducing stress-induced anxiety. The tea's sedative properties also help to slow down rapid heartbeat, allowing the body to regulate its rhythm naturally. Additionally, American basswood's anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate inflammation in the blood vessels, further contributing to a more stable heart rate and reduced palpitations.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: American Basswood tea's high antioxidant content helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to palpitation-related cardiovascular issues.

Calcium-channel blocking activity: The flavonoids present in American Basswood tea have been shown to exhibit calcium-channel blocking properties, which can help regulate heart rhythm and alleviate palpitations caused by irregular heartbeat.

Cardiac glycoside-like effects: American Basswood tea contains cardiac glycosides that resemble digitalis (a traditional heart medication), which may help slow the heart rate, reduce palpitation frequency, and improve overall cardiovascular function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and tannins, which help to calm the nervous system and reduce palpitation symptoms.
Bark: Contains flavonoids and terpenes, which have a sedative effect on the heart and can help regulate abnormal heartbeat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh american basswood leaves and flowers, ensuring proper identification and cleanliness.
Rinse the harvested leaves and flowers with cool water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine one teaspoon of dried american basswood leaves and flowers with eight ounces of boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for seven to ten minutes, then strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable temperature before serving, enjoying as a warm or cold beverage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea teas helps with palpitations because of its unique combination of antioxidants, flavonoids, and caffeine content. The antioxidants in white tea help to relax the blood vessels, reducing inflammation and stress on the heart. The flavonoids, particularly epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), have been shown to slow down the heart rate and reduce palpitation symptoms. Additionally, the moderate amount of caffeine in white tea can help to stimulate the heart without causing jitters or anxiety, providing a sense of calmness and tranquility that can alleviate palpitations.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Herbal white tea's high antioxidant content helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to palpitations by stabilizing heart rhythms and improving overall cardiovascular health.

Cardiac glycosides: Some herbal white teas, such as dandelion and hawthorn, contain cardiac glycosides that can help regulate heart rhythm and improve blood flow, which may alleviate palpitation symptoms.

Calm-inducing compounds: Herbal white tea's calming properties, due to the presence of amino acids like theanine and L-theanine, can help reduce stress and anxiety levels, which are common triggers for palpitations.
remedy_parts: Roots: White tea roots are valued for their calming effects, helping to soothe palpitations caused by stress or anxiety.
Bark: The bark of the white tea plant contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can help regulate heart rhythm and alleviate palpitation symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Measure precisely 2 teaspoons of high-quality white tea leaves.
Heat fresh filtered water to a temperature of 150°f to 160°f.
Pour the heated water over the tea leaves in the infuser.
Strain the tea into a cup using the infuser or strainer,.
Add a squeeze of fresh lemon juice, if desired, and enjoy.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat palpitation with herbal teas is by consuming calming blends that promote relaxation and regulate heartbeat. Hawthorn and valerian root teas are well-known for their soothing properties, reducing anxiety and promoting cardiovascular health. Chamomile and passionflower teas also help alleviate stress and ease heart palpitations. Drink these teas regularly, but remember to consult a healthcare professional before starting any herbal remedy, especially if you're taking medication or have a pre-existing condition.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to palpitation that are treated with herbal teas include rapid heart rate, high blood pressure, and anxiety. For instance, passionflower tea is known to help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and promote relaxation, which can in turn help regulate heart rate. Similarly, hawthorn tea is often used to treat high blood pressure and irregular heartbeat, as it can help dilate blood vessels and improve blood flow. Additionally, chamomile tea has calming properties that can help reduce anxiety and promote better sleep, which can also help regulate heart rate and treat palpitations. However, it's important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal remedies.
